task: I’ve got a promo update that may need to go out even if the weather knocks out power or slows a few locations down. Can you help me think through the safest way to keep the update moving and what to prioritize if parts of the setup become unavailable?

turns.agent_response:
Yes — safest approach is to protect the customer-facing truth first and have a fallback for anything location-dependent.

## Prioritize in this order
1. **Source of truth**
   - landing page
   - promo dates
   - offer
   - availability notes

2. **Customer-facing copy**
   - email
   - partner/store messaging
   - any ad or social text

3. **Location-dependent details**
   - store hours
   - inventory
   - local fulfillment
   - in-person handoff timing

4. **Internal coordination**
   - approvals
   - tracker updates
   - backup contact

---

## If parts become unavailable
### If power/internet is out
- pause anything that can’t be verified
- use the last approved version only if it’s still accurate
- route publishing to the backup owner if needed

### If a location is down or delayed
- remove or soften any location-specific promise
- update hours / availability notes
- notify partner/store contact first
- then internal team
- then any downstream sender

### If inventory is uncertain
- don’t promise stock or same-day availability
- switch to a more general message
- hold any CTA that implies guaranteed fulfillment

---

## What to keep moving
You can usually keep moving:
- internal planning
- draft updates
- non-location-specific messaging
- approved content that doesn’t depend on live availability

---

## What to stop
Pause if:
- the page can’t be verified
- store hours are unknown
- inventory is unconfirmed
- partner copy is stale
- approval is missing
- the backup publisher isn’t available

---

## Quick rule
If the update depends on a location being open, stocked, or reachable, **don’t send until that’s confirmed**.  
If it’s general and customer-safe, you can keep it moving.

If you want, I can turn this into a **weather/facility outage go-no-go checklist** with owner and cutoff columns.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, this is the right direction. But I’d want it tighter and more operational.

What I need next:
- a simple go/no-go checklist
- who owns each step
- what gets paused vs what still ships
- cutoff point for sending if a location goes dark

Also, make it fit a real promo workflow — like:
- customer-facing copy first
- location-specific details last
- backup approver / backup publisher
- what to do if inventory or hours can’t be verified

Basically I want something I can actually use when things get messy, not just general advice.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter, operational version you can use.

# Weather / Outage Promo Go-No-Go

## 1) Order of work
1. **Customer-facing copy**
   - owner: campaign lead
   - includes: offer, dates, CTA, landing page text

2. **Location-specific details**
   - owner: ops / partner contact
   - includes: hours, inventory, fulfillment, store access

3. **Approval**
   - owner: approver
   - backup owner: backup approver

4. **Publish / send**
   - owner: publisher
   - backup owner: backup publisher

---

## 2) Go / No-Go checklist

| Check | Owner | Cutoff | Fallback | Go / No-Go |
|---|---|---:|---|---|
| Customer-facing copy matches approved offer | Campaign lead | T-60 | Hold send | No-go if stale |
| Landing page is live and verified | Publisher | T-45 | Use last approved live page only if accurate | No-go if unverified |
| Hours / location status confirmed | Ops / partner contact | T-30 | Remove location-specific promise | No-go if unknown |
| Inventory / fulfillment confirmed | Ops / partner contact | T-30 | Switch to general message | No-go if uncertain |
| Approval locked | Approver | T-15 | Backup approver signs | No-go if missing |
| Backup publisher confirmed | Publisher | T-15 | Backup publisher takes over | No-go if unavailable |
| Final send list / audience confirmed | Campaign lead | T-10 | Hold send | No-go if unclear |

---

## 3) What gets paused vs what still ships

### Pause
- any location-specific promise
- any store-hour mention
- any inventory-based CTA
- any asset that can’t be verified
- any send if approval is missing

### Can still ship
- general promo copy
- internal planning
- approved assets that don’t depend on live location info
- messaging that remains true even if a location is down

---

## 4) Hard cutoff rule
**If hours, inventory, or location status cannot be verified by T-30, remove the location-specific claim.**  
**If approval or backup publishing is not locked by T-15, no-go.**  
**If the page or copy is stale at send time, pause distribution.**

---

## 5) Who to notify, in order
If a location goes dark or timing shifts:

1. **Backup publisher**
2. **Approver**
3. **Ops / partner contact**
4. **Campaign lead**
5. **Broader internal team / downstream senders**

---

## 6) Simple status note
**Status:** Weather / outage check in progress  
**Current owner:** [name]  
**Blocked by:** [hours / inventory / approval / access]  
**Fallback:** [general copy / last approved page / hold send]  
**Decision by:** [time]

---

## 7) One-line rule
**Customer-facing copy ships first; location-dependent details ship only if they’re verified before cutoff.**

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-screen Slack-ready checklist** with checkboxes and fill-in blanks.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, this is usable. Still want it a little more field-ready though — like something I can paste into Slack or a campaign doc without reworking it.

Need it in a cleaner format:
- 1 short checklist
- owner per line
- explicit go / no-go call
- cutoff times written in plain English
- a dead-simple fallback if hours or inventory go dark

And I’d want the order locked the way real promos run:
- copy first
- landing page second
- location details last
- approvals before send
- backup approver / publisher already named

If you can make it more like a working SOP template, that’s the version I’d actually use.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a **field-ready SOP template** you can paste into Slack or a campaign doc.

---

# Promo Weather / Outage SOP

## Order of work
1. **Customer-facing copy first**  
   Owner: **Campaign lead**

2. **Landing page second**  
   Owner: **Publisher**

3. **Location details last**  
   Owner: **Ops / partner contact**

4. **Approvals before send**  
   Owner: **Approver**  
   Backup: **Backup approver**

5. **Publish / send**  
   Owner: **Publisher**  
   Backup: **Backup publisher**

---

## Go / No-Go checklist

- [ ] **Copy is final and approved**  
  Owner: Campaign lead  
  Cutoff: **by T-60**  
  Fallback: Hold send  
  **Go / No-Go:** No-go if stale

- [ ] **Landing page is live and verified**  
  Owner: Publisher  
  Cutoff: **by T-45**  
  Fallback: Use last approved live page only if accurate  
  **Go / No-Go:** No-go if unverified

- [ ] **Hours / location status confirmed**  
  Owner: Ops / partner contact  
  Cutoff: **by T-30**  
  Fallback: Remove location-specific claim  
  **Go / No-Go:** No-go if unknown

- [ ] **Inventory / fulfillment confirmed**  
  Owner: Ops / partner contact  
  Cutoff: **by T-30**  
  Fallback: Switch to general message  
  **Go / No-Go:** No-go if uncertain

- [ ] **Approval locked**  
  Owner: Approver  
  Cutoff: **by T-15**  
  Fallback: Backup approver signs  
  **Go / No-Go:** No-go if missing

- [ ] **Backup publisher confirmed**  
  Owner: Publisher  
  Cutoff: **by T-15**  
  Fallback: Backup publisher takes over  
  **Go / No-Go:** No-go if unavailable

- [ ] **Audience / send list confirmed**  
  Owner: Campaign lead  
  Cutoff: **by T-10**  
  Fallback: Hold send  
  **Go / No-Go:** No-go if unclear

---

## Dead-simple fallback
If **hours or inventory go dark**:
- remove any location-specific promise
- switch to general promo copy
- keep the landing page only if it’s still accurate
- if not accurate, **hold the send**

---

## Final call
**GO** if:
- copy is final
- page is verified
- hours and inventory are confirmed
- approval is locked
- backup publisher is ready

**NO-GO** if:
- any customer-facing piece is stale
- hours or inventory are unverified
- approval is missing
- backup publisher isn’t confirmed

---

## Who gets pinged if something changes
1. **Backup publisher**
2. **Approver**
3. **Ops / partner contact**
4. **Campaign lead**
5. **Internal team / downstream senders**

If you want, I can turn this into a **single Slack message version** with even fewer words.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah — this works. Clean enough to paste, and the order is right. The cutoff logic is usable too.

The only thing I’d maybe tweak later is making the T-30 / T-15 stuff a little more explicit for people who don’t think in deadlines, but as a working SOP template, this is solid.
